{
  "analysis": "The training data reveals a consistent pattern for German legal texts: Person entities appear in two main contexts. 1) As the subject of a complaint ('Beschwerdesache [Name]') or 'Beschwerde der/des [Name]'. 2) As the judge/official ('durch den Richter/die Richterin [Title] [Name]'). The names often include academic titles (Mag., Dr., Prof., etc.) and suffixes (LL.M., BA, BSc, etc.). The key challenge is distinguishing the person's name from surrounding text like addresses or titles. The pattern generally follows: [Title(s)] [First Name] [Last Name] [Optional Suffix]. The context 'in der Beschwerdesache' or 'durch den Richter/die Richterin' is crucial for disambiguation. Some entries have the name immediately following a preposition like 'des' or 'der'.",
  "strategy": "I will create two primary regex rules. Rule 1 targets names appearing after 'in der Beschwerdesache' or 'der/des' (genitive case) followed by a name pattern. Rule 2 targets names appearing after 'durch den Richter/die Richterin' (or variations like 'Ri', 'R.', etc.) followed by a name pattern. Both rules will use a capture group to extract the name including titles and suffixes, ensuring we capture the full entity text. The name pattern will be flexible to handle multiple titles, first names, last names, and suffixes.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "person_complainant",
      "description": "Extracts person names appearing in the context of a complaint (Beschwerdesache, der/des Beschwerde).",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:in der Beschwerdesache|der|des)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.|\\s+M\\.B\\.L\\.|\\s+MBA|\\s+Bakk\\. iur\\.)*|Mag\\.\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|Dr\\.\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|Hon\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|Priv\\.-Doz\\.(?:in)?\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|Univ\\.-Prof\\.(?:in)?\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|Dipl\\.-Ing\\.(?:in)?\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|ÖkR\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:\\s+LL\\.M\\.|\\s+BA|\\s+BSc|\\s+B\\.A\\.)*|KzlR(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?(?:in)?)(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 5,
      "reasoning": "Captures names following 'Beschwerdesache' or genitive cases like 'der/des'. Handles various titles and suffixes found in the training data."
    },
    {
      "name": "person_judge",
      "description": "Extracts person names appearing in the context of a judge/official ('durch den Richter/die Richterin').",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:durch den Richter|Richter|Ri|Ri1|Ri2|Ri3|Ri4|Ri5|Ri6|Ri7|Ri8|Ri9|Ri10|Ri11|Ri12|Ri13|Ri14|Ri15|Ri16|Ri17|Ri18|Ri19|Ri20|Ri21|Ri22|Ri23|Ri24|Ri25|Ri26|Ri27|Ri28|Ri29|Ri30|Ri31|Ri32|Ri33|Ri34|Ri35|Ri36|Ri37|Ri38|Ri39|Ri40|Ri41|Ri42|Ri43|Ri44|Ri45|Ri46|Ri47|Ri48|Ri49|Ri50|durch die Richterin|RichterinR|RichterinRi|durch die RichterinIBV)\\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?:[A-Z][a-z]+)*(?:,?)(?.*?)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 6,
      "reasoning": "Captures names following judge/official titles. The pattern is simplified to capture the name sequence after the title until a non-name character (like comma or space followed by address). This handles variations like 'Richter', 'Richterin', 'R.', etc."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": []
}

{
  "analysis": "The training data reveals a consistent pattern for German legal documents: Person entities are almost exclusively preceded by specific titles (e.g., 'Mag.', 'Dr.', 'Prof.', 'Richter', 'Frau', 'Herr') or appear in specific syntactic positions (e.g., 'in der Beschwerdesache [Name]'). The entities often include suffixes like degrees ('Bakk. art.', 'MSc') or professional designations ('ÖkR', 'StR'). The main failure mode in generic NER is missing these titles or including the address that follows the name. The rules must prioritize the title+name structure and stop before the address or comma that typically follows.",
  "strategy": "I will create a set of regex rules that target specific title prefixes common in German legal texts. Each rule will use a capture group to isolate the name (including any trailing degrees) while ensuring the match stops before common delimiters like commas, addresses, or prepositions like 'von'. I will use a high priority for specific title patterns (Mag., Dr., Prof.) and a slightly lower priority for generic honorifics (Frau, Herr) to ensure precision.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "academic_and_professional_titles",
      "description": "Matches names preceded by academic titles (Dr., Prof., Mag., Univ.-Prof., Priv.-Doz., Hon.-Prof.) and professional designations (ÖkR, StR, RgR, KommR, MedR, VetR). Handles gendered suffixes like 'in' or 'a'.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Mag\\.|Mag\\.a|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|ÖkR|StR|RgR|KommR|MedR|VetR|PhD|MSc|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|Bakk\\. rer\\. nat\\. MBA)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 10,
      "reasoning": "This is the most common pattern in the training data. It captures the full name including degrees but excludes the title itself. The pattern handles German umlauts and specific degree abbreviations found in the examples."
    },
    {
      "name": "generic_honorifics",
      "description": "Matches names preceded by generic honorifics 'Frau' or 'Herr' which are common in legal descriptions of parties.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Frau|Herr)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 8,
      "reasoning": "Covers cases like 'Frau Jennifer K\u00fcfner' or 'Herr \u00d6kR Samir Foellmer' where the title is a generic honorific rather than a specific academic/professional one."
    },
    {
      "name": "role_based_identification",
      "description": "Matches names following role indicators like 'Beschwerdef\u00fchrer', 'Beschwerdef\u00fchrerin', 'Beschuldigter', 'Schriftf\u00fchrer', 'Liquidator', 'Masseverwalter'.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Beschwerdef\u00fchrer|Beschwerdef\u00fchrerin|Beschuldigter|Schriftf\u00fchrer|Liquidator|Masseverwalter)(?:in)?(?:,|=|\\s+)([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 7,
      "reasoning": "In legal texts, parties are often introduced by their role. This rule captures the name immediately following the role definition."
    },
    {
      "name": "post_position_names",
      "description": "Matches names appearing after prepositions like 'von', 'des', 'der' in contexts like 'von der Firma X' or 'des X Y' where X Y is a person.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:von|des|der)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)+)(?:,|\\s|$)",
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "priority": 5,
      "reasoning": "Covers cases where the name appears after a preposition, often in phrases like 'von der Firma' (if the firm is named after a person) or 'des X Y'. This is less specific but catches edge cases."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": []
}

{
  "analysis": "The primary failures stem from two issues: 1) The 'academic_and_professional_titles' rule is too greedy, matching the title (e.g., 'Mag.', 'Dr.') but failing to include it in the output text, and often matching the title without the name if the name is missing or malformed. 2) The 'post_position_names' rule is extremely broad, matching any phrase following 'Beschwerdesache' or similar context, which incorrectly captures the entire phrase including the entity, and also incorrectly matches organization names (e.g., 'Finanzamt...') because they appear in similar syntactic positions. The 'generic_honorifics' rule is working correctly for 'Frau/Herr' cases but needs to be prioritized or kept distinct.",
  "strategy": "1. Replace 'academic_and_professional_titles' with a set of specific, high-priority rules that match known German legal titles (Mag., Dr., Univ.-Prof., Priv.-Doz., ÖkR, etc.) followed by the full name. These rules will capture the title + name as a single entity. 2. Replace 'post_position_names' with a specific rule that matches the pattern 'in der Beschwerdesache [Name]' or 'in der Verwaltungsstrafsache [Name]' but strictly limits the capture to the name part, ensuring it doesn't capture the preceding context or subsequent address details. 3. Ensure 'generic_honorifics' remains but is not the primary source of errors. 4. Add a rule for 'RgR' (Rechtsanwaltsanwärter) and 'StR' (Staatsanwalt) which appeared in failures.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "academic_and_professional_titles",
      "description": "Matches German legal and academic titles (Mag., Dr., Univ.-Prof., Priv.-Doz., ÖkR, etc.) followed by the full name.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|ÖkR|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|PhD|StR|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|BA|RgR)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)",
      "priority": 9,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "This rule captures the title and the name together, fixing the issue where only the name was extracted. It uses specific known titles from the training data to avoid false positives on generic words."
    },
    {
      "name": "post_position_names",
      "description": "Matches names appearing after specific legal case context phrases like 'in der Beschwerdesache' or 'in der Verwaltungsstrafsache'.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:in der Beschwerdesache|in der Verwaltungsstrafsache)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*(?:,\\s*[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "This rule specifically targets the 'in der [case_type] [Name]' pattern. It captures only the name part ($1) to avoid including the context phrase. It is more specific than the previous version which matched too broadly."
    },
    {
      "name": "generic_honorifics",
      "description": "Matches names preceded by generic honorifics 'Frau' or 'Herr' which are common in legal descriptions of parties.",
      "format": "regex",
      "pattern": "(?:Frau|Herr)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)",
      "priority": 7,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities",
      "reasoning": "Kept as is, but lower priority than specific titles. It correctly handles cases like 'Frau Arielle Bungert'."
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": [
    "academic_and_professional_titles",
    "post_position_names"
  ]
}

{
  "analysis": "The failures indicate three main issues: 1) The 'post_position_names' rule is too greedy, capturing addresses (e.g., 'Großmeiseldorfer', 'Am Klauberpark') and truncating names (e.g., 'Vet' instead of 'VetR Konrad Pfähler'). It lacks a stop condition for addresses. 2) The 'academic_and_professional_titles' rule fails to capture names when the title is followed by a comma or specific context, or when the title is part of a longer phrase like 'Richter Mag. ...'. 3) Several cases (e.g., 'Flora Detgens' after 'der Beschwerdeführer', 'OSR Valentina Fruendt') are missed because the rules rely on specific role keywords that don't cover all variations (e.g., 'des Beschwerdeführers' vs 'der Beschwerdeführer'). The 'generic_honorifics' rule is also too broad and misses names without 'Herr/Frau'.",
  "strategy": "1. Replace 'post_position_names' with a stricter rule that stops at commas followed by street indicators (Straße, Gasse, etc.) or numbers, and handles titles like 'VetR' correctly. 2. Enhance 'academic_and_professional_titles' to handle titles followed by commas or specific prepositions, and ensure it captures the full name including suffixes. 3. Add a new rule for 'role_based_identification' that covers genitive cases (e.g., 'des Beschwerdeführers') and other variations. 4. Add a rule for names appearing after 'der/die/den' + role without a comma, or in direct address contexts. 5. Ensure 'VetR' and similar abbreviations are handled as part of the name or title context.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "post_position_names",
      "description": "Matches names following 'in der Beschwerdesache' or similar, stopping before address indicators.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:in der Beschwerdesache|in der Verwaltungsstrafsache)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*(?:\\s+(?:VetR|OSR|StR|Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|PhD|ÖkR|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|BA|RgR|KommR|MSc|Mag\\.a))?\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "role_based_identification",
      "description": "Matches names following role indicators like 'Beschwerdeführer', 'Beschwerdeführerin', 'Beschuldigter', 'Schriftführer', 'Liquidator', 'Masseverwalter', handling case variations and genitive forms.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:des\\s+Beschwerdeführers|der\\s+Beschwerdeführerin|des\\s+Beschwerdeführers|des\\s+Beschuldigten|der\\s+Beschuldigten|des\\s+Schriftführers|der\\s+Schriftführerin|des\\s+Liquidators|des\\s+Masseverwalters|des\\s+Masseverwalters)(?:,|=|\\s+)([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)",
      "priority": 7,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "academic_and_professional_titles",
      "description": "Matches German legal and academic titles followed by the full name, handling complex titles and suffixes, including titles after 'Richter' or 'Richterin'.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:Richter|Richterin)\\s+(?:Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|ÖkR|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|PhD|StR|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|BA|RgR|KommR|MSc|Mag\\.a|VetR|OSR)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)",
      "priority": 9,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "academic_and_professional_titles_general",
      "description": "Matches German legal and academic titles followed by the full name in general contexts (not after Richter/Richterin).",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|ÖkR|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|PhD|StR|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|BA|RgR|KommR|MSc|Mag\\.a|VetR|OSR)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)",
      "priority": 9,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "generic_honorifics",
      "description": "Matches names preceded by generic honorifics 'Frau' or 'Herr' which are common in legal descriptions of parties.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:Frau|Herr)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)",
      "priority": 7,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "names_after_role_context",
      "description": "Matches names appearing after specific legal case context phrases where the name is not immediately preceded by a title, stopping before address indicators.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:in der Beschwerdesache|in der Verwaltungsstrafsache)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "names_with_suffixes",
      "description": "Matches names that have suffixes like MSc, Bakk. art. MA, BA, etc., appearing after the name or as part of the entity.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)(?:,\\s*(?:MSc|Bakk\\. art\\. MA|BA|RgR|StR|PhD))",
      "priority": 6,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$0",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    }
  ],
  "deleted_rules": [
    "post_position_names",
    "role_based_identification",
    "academic_and_professional_titles",
    "generic_honorifics",
    "names_with_suffixes"
  ]
}

{
  "analysis": "The primary failure mode is the inability to detect names appearing in isolation (no preceding title, role, or specific context phrase). The training data shows 'Guntram Mittendorf' appearing frequently as a standalone subject or object (e.g., 'Nachdem Guntram Mittendorf...', 'von Guntram Mittendorf...'). The existing rules rely heavily on context like 'Herr', 'Dr.', 'Beschwerdeführer', or specific phrases like 'in der Beschwerdesache'. The rule 'names_after_role_context' is too specific to only two phrases. The rule 'academic_and_professional_titles_general' is too broad and misses names without titles. The rule 'generic_honorifics' misses names without 'Herr/Frau'. The pattern `([A-Z]...)(?:\\s+(?:VetR|...))?` captures the name and optional title, but the expected output includes the full string 'Zlatan Grensenbach, MA Bakk. art.'. **Action**: The pattern needs to capture the comma and the subsequent degrees. **Fix**: Change the capture group to `([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*(?:\\s*,\\s*(?:MA|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.\\s*MA|...)?))` to include the comma and the full list of degrees.",
    "f6ffc90d": "CRITICAL FIX: Matches 'Elfriede Löwy  ' (with trailing spaces). **Action**: Add a word boundary or non-whitespace anchor at the end of the capture group to prevent trailing spaces: `([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)(?=\\s*(?:,|\\.|\\n|$))`."
  },
  "task_guidance": "1. **Consolidate Title Handling**: You have 4 rules (`e66eaaf6`, `5be06c03`, `f6ffc90d`, `b3eebc61`) that all try to handle titles. This causes fragmentation. Create a single, robust 'Title + Name' pattern that captures the full entity (Title + Name + Degrees) and use it as the primary rule. 2. **Contextual Filtering**: The system is failing to distinguish between 'Judges' (Richter) and 'Parties' (Beschwerdeführer). In German legal texts, the judge's name often appears in a passive construction ('Das Gericht hat durch den Richter X...'). You must add a negative context rule to exclude names immediately following 'Richter', 'Richterin', 'Schriftführer', etc., unless they are explicitly marked as the party. 3. **Anchor and Boundary Fixes**: All rules with trailing spaces or partial matches (like 'Rg', 'Bf') need strict word boundaries (`\\b`) and end-of-string/punctuation anchors. 4. **Priority Strategy**: The current priority system is not resolving conflicts. The 'names_with_titles_general' rule (Priority 9) is running before 'names_after_role_context' (Priority 10) in some cases, but the logic is flawed. Ensure that the most specific rule (e.g., 'Judge Name' vs 'Party Name') has the highest priority, or use a 'negative' rule to filter out judges first. 5. **Data Alignment**: Ensure the expected output format matches the regex capture groups. If the expected output includes titles, the regex must capture them. If not, the regex should not capture them."

metadata.analysis: The system suffers from catastrophic over-extraction of titles and abbreviations as person names due to regex patterns that capture the text *following* a title rather than the title itself, and a failure to distinguish between the judge's name (often in a passive construction) and the parties involved. The rules are also fragmented, causing the same entity to be split into multiple partial matches (e.g., 'Rg' and 'Peter Schlossnagel').

{
  "analysis": "The primary failures stem from two issues: 1) The 'names_in_complaint_context' rule is too greedy, capturing trailing commas and whitespace (e.g., 'Agnes Martinu,  ') because it doesn't explicitly stop at punctuation. 2) The 'names_with_titles_general' and 'names_with_complex_titles' rules are capturing trailing whitespace (e.g., 'Dr. Elisabeth Hafner ') because the regex allows optional whitespace at the end of the name pattern without a strict boundary. 3) Several cases (e.g., 'Hugo Buring', 'Prof.in Tamara Simanek' in non-complaint contexts like 'bei der Hugo Buring') are missed because the existing rules rely heavily on specific prepositions ('in der Beschwerdesache') or role indicators ('Beschwerdeführer') which are absent in these contexts. The pattern 'bei der [Name]' is a common legal construction for service of process that needs explicit handling.",
  "strategy": "1. Tighten 'names_in_complaint_context' to stop before commas or whitespace by using a negative lookahead or explicit character class for the end of the match. 2. Tighten 'names_with_titles_general' and 'names_with_complex_titles' to ensure they do not capture trailing whitespace by adding a word boundary or non-whitespace assertion at the end. 3. Add a new rule 'names_after_preposition_context' to capture names following prepositions like 'bei der', 'an die', 'mit der' which are common in legal texts for service of process or contact, specifically targeting the missed 'Hugo Buring' and 'Prof.in Tamara Simanek' cases. 4. Ensure all rules use $1 for the name capture to avoid capturing surrounding context, or $0 with strict boundaries.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "names_in_complaint_context",
      "description": "Matches names appearing immediately after 'in der Beschwerdesache' or 'in der Rechtsmittelsache', capturing the full name including optional academic titles, stopping before punctuation.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:in\\s+der\\s+(?:Beschwerde|Rechtsmittel)sache(?:\\s+der)?\\s+)([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*(?:\\s+von\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)?(?:\\s*,\\s*(?:LLM|LLB|MSc|BEd|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Dr\\.|MedR|StR|PhD|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.\\s*MA|BA|RgR|KommR|VetR|OSR|\\u00d6kR|Bakk\\.\\s*techn\\.)?)?)",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "names_with_titles_general",
      "description": "Matches names with specific legal/academic titles (Dr., Mag., OStR, etc.) ensuring the FULL title+name is captured, with improved handling for multi-word titles and trimmed whitespace.",

{
  "analysis": "The system suffers from severe fragmentation where complex titles are split into multiple entities (e.g., 'Mag.a Priv' and 'Hon.-Prof.in...') and abbreviations are incorrectly identified as names (e.g., 'Rg', 'Komm').     "11dd921d": "This rule is the primary source of fragmentation and false positives. It also matches non-person titles like 'Mag.a Priv' in isolation. **Action**: Rewrite the pattern to be greedy for titles. Use a non-capturing group for the title sequence `(?:Title1|Title2|...)+` followed by the name. Add a negative lookahead `(?![\n,])` or ensure the match ends with a name character, not a title abbreviation. Specifically, handle the 'Mag.a Priv.-Doz.in' case by allowing multiple consecutive title tokens before the name.",

If 'Betrifft' headers are handled elsewhere, remove this. If needed, add a constraint that the name must be followed by a newline or end-of-string, and explicitly exclude single-letter or two-letter matches. Consider removing this rule entirely if 'names_in_complaint_context' covers the necessary ground.",
    "622c81c7": "This rule incorrectly identifies 'Abgabenbehörde' (Tax Authority) as a person. **Action**: Add a negative lookahead `(?!(?:Behörde|Amt|Gericht|Kammer))` to the capture group to exclude common organizational nouns.     "f6ffc90d": "This rule produces 0% precision and matches trailing whitespace or partial names ('Elfriede Löwy  '). **Action**: The pattern is too loose. Add a strict end-of-match anchor or ensure the capture group stops at the first non-name character (like a comma or period) and does not include trailing spaces. It seems to be capturing the name but including the whitespace in the span.",

    "0ac55225": "This rule has 0% recall, meaning it fails to find the expected entities. **Action**: The pattern requires a specific title immediately after the name, but the data often has the title before the name or no title at all. Relax the pattern to make the title optional or move the title matching to a separate rule that runs before this one.",
    "f96d0da4": "This rule matches 'So' (part of 'Sohn') and 'Familienbeihilfe' (part of a compound noun). **Action**: Add word boundaries `\\b` around the family terms and ensure the capture group requires a valid name structure (Capitalized + lowercase). Exclude common non-name words that might follow family terms.",
    "b3eebc61": "This rule misses the full entity span (e.g., 'Zlatan Grensenbach, MA Bakk. art.' becomes just 'Zlatan Grensenbach'). **Action**: Extend the pattern to capture the optional degree suffixes after the name, similar to the fix for `3393d45b`. The current pattern stops at the name, missing the qualification.",
    "2947718a": "Same issue as `3393d45b`: missing the degree suffix. **Action**: Append the optional degree pattern to the end of the capture group to include 'BEd', 'MA', etc., ensuring the full entity is returned.",
    "generic_honorifics": "Duplicate of `3393d45b` and `2947718a` (likely a naming confusion in the list). **Action**: Consolidate these rules. If they are distinct, ensure they don't overlap. If they are the same, remove the duplicate to reduce confusion and processing time."

  "task_guidance": "1. **Consolidate Title Matching**: The current approach treats titles and names as separate or loosely connected entities. You need a 'Title-Name' atomic rule that greedily matches all consecutive titles (e.g., 'Mag.a Priv.-Doz.in') followed by the name. This will fix the fragmentation issue (e.g., 'Mag.a Priv' + 'Hon.-Prof.in...').\n2. **Implement Negative Constraints**: Many False Positives ('Rg', 'Komm', 'Abgabenbehörde') occur because the rules match any capitalized sequence. You must add negative lookaheads to exclude known non-person entities (roles, organizations, abbreviations) and ensure the match contains at least one lowercase letter.\n3. **Priority Reordering**: Currently, rules with lower priority (e.g., `names_in_complaint_context` at 9) are running after more specific rules, but they are too broad. The 'Title-Name' rule (currently `11dd921d`) should have the **highest priority** (e.g., 10 or 11) to capture the full entity first, preventing other rules from matching partial fragments.\n4. **Contextual Disambiguation**: Add a rule or logic to detect 'Court/Role' contexts (e.g., 'durch den Richter') and explicitly exclude the role name itself from being captured as a person, or ensure the name follows the role immediately.\n5. **Remove Redundant Rules**: `generic_honorifics`, `names_after_herr_frau`, and `3393d45b` appear to be duplicates or near-duplicates. Consolidate them into a single robust rule to reduce conflict and improve maintainability."

{
  "analysis": "The primary failure mode is the 'names_with_complex_titles' rule (ID 11dd921d) which is too broad. It matches academic titles (Dr., Mag., etc.) followed by a capitalized word, but fails to verify that the word is a surname. This causes false positives like 'Dr. Gabriele' (matching only the first name) or 'Mag. Anna' (matching only the first name) when the full name is 'Mag. Anna Mechtler-Höger'. Additionally, the rule incorrectly captures 'Rechtsanwalts' (part of a firm name) as a person. The 'role_based_identification' rule works for 'Melanie Thomzigk' but fails for names appearing without role indicators (e.g., 'Frederik Ullmann' at the start of a sentence). The 'names_in_complaint_context' rule works for 'Hugo Refus' but fails for 'Jelena Eibert' when the context is slightly different or when the name is followed by an address immediately. The core issue is the lack of a robust pattern for 'Title + First Name + Surname' where the Surname is required to be a capitalized word, and the rule must stop before non-name words (like 'Rechtsanwalts').",
  "strategy": "1. Replace 'names_with_complex_titles' with a stricter pattern that requires a Title (Dr., Mag., etc.) followed by a First Name and a Surname (at least two capitalized words after the title). This prevents matching 'Dr. Gabriele' alone. 2. Add a negative lookahead or specific exclusion for common non-person words following the name (e.g., 'Rechtsanwalts', 'GmbH', 'AG'). 3. Add a new rule 'standalone_names' to capture names at the start of sentences or after common verbs/prepositions where no title is present, using a pattern that requires a capitalized First Name followed by a capitalized Surname. 4. Refine 'names_in_complaint_context' to ensure it captures the full name even if followed by an address, by stopping at punctuation or whitespace before the address.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "names_with_complex_titles",
      "description": "Matches names preceded by academic titles (Dr., Mag., etc.) ensuring at least a First and Last name are present, excluding firm names.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:Dr\\.|Mag\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Bakk\\.\\s*(?:phil\\.|techn\\.|art\\.|jur\\.?)?|LLM|LLB|MSc|BEd|PhD|MedR|StR|RgR|KommR|VetR|OSR|ÖkR)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)*)\\s+([A-Z][a-zäöüß]+)(?!\\s+(?:Rechtsanwalts|GmbH|AG|Partnerschaft|Steuerberatung|Wirtschaftstreuhand|Gesellschaft))",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1 $2",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "standalone_names",
      "description": "Captures names appearing at the start of a sentence or after common verbs/prepositions without titles, requiring First + Last name.",
      "format": "regex",

metadata.guidance: Prioritize reducing the high false positive rate (1350 FP) by refining negative constraints to exclude non-person objects currently misclassified. Focus on adding specific spatial or contextual featur

messages.content:
{
  "analysis": "The failures reveal three main issues: 1) The 'names_with_complex_titles' rule incorrectly captures judges/officials (e.g., 'Mag. Josef Zwilling') when the context is 'durch den Richter' (by the judge), mistaking the official for the party. 2) The 'generic_honorifics' rule incorrectly includes the honorific 'Frau' or 'Herr' in the entity text (e.g., 'Frau Cornelia Hofstett' instead of 'Cornelia Hofstett') and fails to capture the full suffix 'BA LLB' correctly in some contexts. 3) The 'names_in_complaint_context' and 'names_in_representative_context' rules are missing specific patterns for 'des' (genitive) or 'für' (for) contexts, leading to missed entities like 'Frederike Jaks' in 'des Frederike Jaks' or 'für Frederike Jaks'. 4) The 'names_at_start_of_text' rule is too broad, matching 'Universität Wien' or 'Begründung' when they are not names.",
  "strategy": "1. Modify 'names_with_complex_titles' to explicitly exclude names following 'Richter', 'Richterin', 'durch den', 'durch die' to prevent capturing the judge. 2. Modify 'generic_honorifics' to capture only the name part ($1) and ensure it handles the 'BA LLB' suffix correctly. 3. Add/Update 'names_in_complaint_context' to handle 'des' (genitive) and 'für' (for) prepositions. 4. Add a specific rule for 'names_after_preposition_context' to handle 'für' (for) and 'des' (of) in legal contexts. 5. Tighten 'names_at_start_of_text' to require a comma immediately after the name to avoid matching non-names.",
  "rules": [
    {
      "name": "names_with_complex_titles",
      "description": "Matches names preceded by 'Richter' or 'Richterin' and optional titles, capturing ONLY the title and name, excluding the role word. EXCLUDES names following 'durch den/die' which are judges.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?<!durch\\s+(?:den|die|der|des)\\s+)(?:Richter|Richterin)\\s+(?:(?:Ing\\.\\s+)?(?:Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Mag\\.|Mag\\.a|Mag\\.Dr\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|Techn\\s+R\\.|Dkfm\\.|Wirtschaftstreuh\\u00e4nder\\.|Wirtschaftspr\\u00fcfer\\.|KommR|VetR|OSR|\\u00d6kR|StR|MedR|LLM|Bakk\\.\\s*techn\\.|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.|Bakk\\.\\s*iur\\.)\\s+)?([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+(?:von\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?)?)",
      "priority": 10,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "generic_honorifics",
      "description": "Matches names preceded by 'Herr' or 'Frau', capturing ONLY the name (excluding the honorific) and full suffixes like 'BA LLB'.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:Herr|Frau)\\s+(?:(?:Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Mag\\.|Mag\\.a|Mag\\.Dr\\.|Ing\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|Techn\\s+R\\.|Dkfm\\.|Wirtschaftstreuh\\u00e4nder\\.|Wirtschaftspr\\u00fcfer\\.|KommR|VetR|OSR|\\u00d6kR|StR|MedR|LLM|BEd|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.|Bakk\\.\\s*techn\\.|Bakk\\.\\s*iur\\.|MSc|LLB|Dipl\\.|MBA|PhD|BA|RgR)\\s+)?([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+von\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?)(?:\\s*,\\s*(?:LLM|LLB|MSc|BEd|Mag\\.a|Dr\\.in|Dr\\.|MedR|StR|PhD|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.|Bakk\\.\\s*techn\\.|Bakk\\.\\s*iur\\.|MA|BA|RgR|KommR|VetR|OSR|\\u00d6kR)?)?",
      "priority": 8,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "names_in_complaint_context",
      "description": "Matches names appearing after 'in der Beschwerdesache' or similar, capturing ONLY the name and titles, excluding the context phrase. Handles 'des' (genitive) and 'für' (for) contexts.",
      "format": "regex",
      "content": "(?:in\\s+der\\s+(?:Beschwerde|Rechtsmittel|Revision|Verwaltungsstraf|Finanzstraf)sache(?:\\s+(?:des|der))?\\s+|des\\s+(?:Beschwerdeführers|Beschwerdeführerin|Beschuldigten|Beschuldigten|Schriftführers|Schriftführerin|Liquidators|Masseverwalters)\\s+|für\\s+(?:den|die|das)\\s+(?:Beschwerdeführer|Beschwerdeführerin|Beschuldigten|Beschuldigte|Schriftführer|Schriftführerin|Liquidator|Masseverwalter)\\s+)(?:(?:KzlR|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Mag\\.|Mag\\.a|Mag\\.Dr\\.|VetR|OSR|StR|\\u00d6kR|KommR|MSc|LLM|LLB|BEd|Dipl\\.|MBA|Techn\\s+R\\.|Dkfm\\.|Wirtschaftstreuh\\u00e4nder\\.|Wirtschaftspr\\u00fcfer\\.|MedR|PhD|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.\\s*MA|BA|RgR)\\s+)?([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+(?:in\\s+der|von\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*)",
      "priority": 9,
      "output_template": {
        "text": "$1",
        "start": "$start",
        "end": "$end",
        "type": "person"
      },
      "output_key": "entities"
    },
    {
      "name": "names_in_representative_context",

metadata.analysis: The system suffers from severe over-extraction due to overly permissive regex patterns that match common nouns (e.g., 'Jahre', 'Verl') and partial titles as person names, resulting in a 43% precision rate. Additionally, the ruleset lacks robust context-awareness to distinguish between the names of judges (who appear in 'recognizes by' phrases) and the parties involved in the case, leading to high false positives and missed entities with complex titles.

You are the Refinement Coordinator for a rule learning system.
After each refinement iteration, you analyze per-class performance and guide the next patch.

ITERATION: 1/1
OVERALL: accuracy=16.9%, micro_F1=45.5%, macro_F1=45.5%

PER-CLASS PERFORMANCE (sorted worst to best):
  person: F1=45% P=43% R=49% (TP=595 FP=801 FN=626)

Return JSON:
{
  "focus_classes": ["list of class names that need the most improvement"],
  "guidance": "Specific advice for the rule generator — which classes to prioritize, what patterns to try, what to avoid. 2-3 sentences max.",
  "should_continue": boolean (false if performance is good enough or unlikely to improve further)
}

You are updating an existing rule-based extractor. Do NOT rewrite good rules; add or adjust only what is needed.

Task: German Legal Named Entity Recognition
Description: Recognize named entities in German legal text. Entities to look for: person.

Input schema: {'text': 'str'}
Output schema:
entities: List[Entity]

Entity:
  text: string  # The matched text span
  start: integer  # Start character offset
  end: integer  # End character offset
  type: string  # Entity label
        
AVAILABLE ENTITY TYPES: ['person']
Rules MUST use one of these types in output_template.


DATA EVIDENCE FROM TRAINING:
- person (15 unique): "Rudolf Hummelt", "Hon.-Prof. Lothar D\u00f6ppkens", "Claire Strotmeyer", "Dr. Irvin Math\u00e4ss", "Zlatan Grensenbach, MA Bakk. art.", "Paul Br\u00e4udigam", "RgR PhD Franka Kopecky", "Mag.a Priv.-Doz.in Hon.-Prof.in Melanie Kreikemeier", "PhD Sascha Bl\u00fcmke", "Justin Feuerheerdt", "Mag. Mag. Hon.-Prof. Gottfried Rabensteiner", "KommR KzlR Finn H\u00e4rtich", "Urs Schelzel", "RgR Peter Schlossnagel", "Matth\u00e4us Marsteller"
  Exact pattern: (?:Ma(?:g\.(?:a Priv\.\-Doz\.in Hon\.\-Prof\.in Melanie Kreikemei| Mag\. Hon\.\-Prof\. Gottfried Rabenstein)er|tthäus Marsteller)|Zlatan Grensenbach, MA Bakk\. art\.|Hon\.\-Prof\. Lothar Döppkens|KommR KzlR Finn Härtich|RgR P(?:eter Schlossnagel|hD Franka Kopecky)|Justin Feuerheerdt|P(?:hD Sascha Blümke|aul Bräudigam)|Claire Strotmeyer|Dr\. Irvin Mathäss|Rudolf Hummelt|Urs Schelzel)
  In context: "... Beschwerdesache [Rudolf Hummelt], Zuberbach-Neuhof...", "...durch den Richter [Hon.-Prof. Lothar D\u00f6ppkens]  in der...", "...Beschwerdesache [Claire Strotmeyer],  Am Liding 12,...", "...durch den Richter [Dr. Irvin Math\u00e4ss]  in der...", "...Beschwerdesache [Zlatan Grensenbach, MA Bakk. art.], ..."

Note: Some entities contain multiple words; do NOT assume single-token matches.

Computed patterns match training strings only; generalize carefully.

USER GUIDANCE (task-level feedback):
- 1. **Consolidate Duplicate Rules**: You have multiple rules with identical or near-identical patterns (e.g., 'names_in_complaint_context' vs 'names_in_case_context', 'names_with_herr_frau' vs 'names_after_herr'). Merge these into single, robust rules to reduce noise and simplify priority management.
2. **Contextual Disambiguation**: The system fails to distinguish between the 'Judge' (who recognizes the case) and the 'Party' (the person in the case). In German legal texts, phrases like 'Das Bundesfinanzgericht erkennt durch die Richterin [Name]' refer to the judge, not the party. You need a specific rule or negative context to exclude names appearing immediately after 'erkennt durch' or similar judicial phrases.
3. **Title Handling**: The current rules often capture partial titles (e.g., 'RgR Ph') as names. Implement a 'Title-Name-Title' pattern that captures the entire sequence (e.g., 'Dr. Irvin Mathäss, MA') as a single entity, rather than stopping at the first space.
4. **Negative Constraints**: Add negative lookaheads to exclude common German nouns that start with a capital letter (e.g., 'Jahre', 'Monat', 'Tag', 'Verl') from being matched as names. This will drastically reduce false positives.
5. **Priority Strategy**: Ensure that rules capturing full names with titles (e.g., 'Dr. Name') have higher priority than rules capturing partial names or just the name part. This ensures the most complete entity is selected.


COORDINATOR GUIDANCE (prioritize this):
Prioritize refining rules for the 'person' class to address the high false positive rate (801) which is dragging down precision. Focus on adding negative constraints or more specific contextual features to distinguish persons from similar-looking objects, while ensuring recall does not drop further.


PER-CLASS METRICS (current performance):
  person: P=43% R=49% F1=45% (TP=595 FP=801 FN=626)

RULES with failures (full details, note any user_feedback on specific rules):
[
  {
    "name": "role_based_identification",
    "description": "Matches names following role indicators like 'Beschwerdef\u00fchrer', 'Beschuldigter', etc., ensuring the name is captured fully.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "(?:des\\s+Beschwerdef\u00fchrers|der\\s+Beschwerdef\u00fchrerin|des\\s+Beschuldigten|der\\s+Beschuldigten|des\\s+Schriftf\u00fchrers|der\\s+Schriftf\u00fchrerin|des\\s+Liquidators|des\\s+Masseverwalters|des\\s+Masseverwalters)(?:,|=|\\s+)([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+(?:von|van)\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?)(?:\\s+(?:VetR|OSR|StR|Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|PhD|\\u00d6kR|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.\\s*MA|BA|RgR|KommR|MSc|Mag\\.a|LLM|LLB|BEd|Dipl\\.|MBA|OSR|\\u00d6kR|KommR|VetR|Techn\\s+R\\.|Dkfm\\.|Wirtschaftstreuh\\u00e4nder\\.|Wirtschaftspr\\u00fcfer\\.|MedR|Bakk\\.\\s*techn\\.|Bakk\\.\\s*iur\\.)?)",
    "priority": 7,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "The pattern for 'role_based_identification' is too greedy. It matches the role (e.g., 'des Beschwerdef\u00fchrers') followed by a name, but the name capture group is too loose, allowing it to match non-names like 'Verl' or partial words. Add a strict word boundary check after the name and ensure the preceding role is strictly followed by a comma, equals sign, or whitespace, not just any character."
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "names_in_headers",
    "description": "Matches names appearing in headers or subject lines, often after colons or hyphens.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "(?:Betrifft|Betreff|Name|Antragsteller|Beschwerdef\u00fchrer|Beschwerdef\u00fchrerin)\\s*[:\\-]\\s*([A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+von\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df]+)?)",
    "priority": 8,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "The 'names_in_headers' rule is matching the entire header line or partial words (e.g., 'Bf', 'Strafe Guten') because the capture group is too broad and lacks a negative lookahead to exclude non-name tokens. Restrict the capture group to only match sequences of capitalized words that look like names (e.g., exclude single letters or common nouns) and ensure the separator is strictly a colon or dash."
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "names_with_complex_titles",
    "description": "Matches names preceded by 'Richter' or 'Richterin' and optional titles, capturing ONLY the title and name, excluding the role word. Handles abbreviations like 'Ri' correctly by requiring a title or full name.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "(?:Richter|Richterin)\\s+(?:(?:Ing\\.\\s+)?(?:Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Mag\\.|Mag\\.a|Mag\\.Dr\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Techn\\s+R\\.|Dkfm\\.|Wirtschaftstreuh\\u00e4nder\\.|Wirtschaftspr\\u00fcfer\\.|KommR|VetR|OSR|\\u00d6kR|StR|MedR|LLM|Bakk\\.\\s*techn\\.|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.|Bakk\\.\\s*iur\\.)\\s+)?([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+(?:von\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?)?)",
    "priority": 10,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "This rule is the primary source of false positives (524 FP). It matches any word following 'Richter' or 'Richterin' even if it's a title or a common noun. The pattern must be tightened to require a specific title sequence (e.g., 'Dr.', 'Mag.') immediately before the name, or the name must be followed by a comma or end of sentence, not just any whitespace."
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "names_after_preposition_context",
    "description": "Matches names following prepositions like 'bei der', 'an die', 'mit der', 'f\u00fcr' which are common in legal texts for service of process or contact, capturing the full name.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "(?:bei\\s+der|an\\s+die|mit\\s+der|zur\\s+Zustellung\\s+an\\s+die|an\\s+den|bei\\s+den|f\\u00fcr\\s+(?:den|die|das)\\s+)([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+von\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?)(?=,|\\s+\\d+|\\s+\\w+\\s+\\d+|\\s+\\w+\\s+\\w+\\s+\\d+|\\s*$)",
    "priority": 7,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "The 'names_after_preposition_context' rule is matching common nouns like 'Jahre' (years) because the pattern `[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df]+` is too generic. Add a constraint that the matched name must be at least 2 characters long and exclude common German nouns that start with a capital letter (e.g., 'Jahre', 'Monat', 'Tag') using a negative lookahead or a whitelist of valid name patterns."
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "names_in_complaint_context_general",
    "description": "Matches names appearing after 'in der Beschwerdesache' or 'in der Revisionssache' or 'in der Finanzstrafsache' or 'in der Verwaltungsstrafsache', capturing the full name including titles, stopping strictly before address indicators or commas.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "(?:in\\s+der\\s+(?:Beschwerde|Rechtsmittel|Revision|Finanzstraf|Verwaltungsstraf)sache(?:\\s+(?:des|der))?\\s+)([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+(?:von\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?)?(?:\\s+(?:VetR|OSR|StR|Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|PhD|\\u00d6kR|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.\\s*MA|BA|RgR|KommR|MSc|Mag\\.a|LLM|LLB|BEd|Dipl\\.|MBA|OSR|\\u00d6kR|KommR|VetR|Techn\\s+R\\.|Dkfm\\.|Wirtschaftstreuh\\u00e4nder\\.|Wirtschaftspr\\u00fcfer\\.|MedR)?)?)",
    "priority": 9,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "Similar to 'names_in_complaint_context_general', this rule is capturing partial titles (e.g., 'Rg', 'Ph') instead of full names. The pattern needs to enforce that the captured group is a full name, not just the start of a title. Add a requirement that the name must be followed by a comma, a number, or a specific title suffix, and ensure the capture group doesn't stop prematurely."
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "names_after_gegen",
    "description": "Matches names appearing after 'gegen' in legal contexts, specifically looking for capitalized names that are likely persons, excluding common legal nouns.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "(?:gegen\\s+)([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+(?:van|von)\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?)(?:\\s+(?:VetR|OSR|StR|Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|PhD|\\u00d6kR|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.\\s*MA|BA|RgR|KommR|MSc|Mag\\.a|LLM|LLB|BEd|Dipl\\.|MBA|OSR|\\u00d6kR|KommR|VetR|Techn\\s+R\\.|Dkfm\\.|Wirtschaftstreuh\\u00e4nder\\.|Wirtschaftspr\\u00fcfer\\.|MedR|Bakk\\.\\s*techn\\.|Bakk\\.\\s*iur\\.)?)",
    "priority": 12,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "The 'names_after_gegen' rule is matching first names only (e.g., 'Jolanda', 'Massimo') when the full name is expected. The pattern should require a second capitalized word (surname) or a title to follow the first name to avoid partial matches. Alternatively, if first names are valid, ensure the context strictly implies a person (e.g., 'gegen [Name] [Nachname]')."
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "names_at_start_of_text",
    "description": "Matches names appearing at the very beginning of the text, followed by a comma (common in case headers). Excludes non-person words.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "^([A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+(?:von\\s+[A-Z][a-z\u00e4\u00f6\u00fc\u00df]+)?)?(?:\\s+(?:VetR|OSR|StR|Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|PhD|\u00d6kR|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.\\s*MA|BA|RgR|KommR|MSc|Mag\\.a|LLM|LLB|BEd|Dipl\\.|MBA|OSR|\u00d6kR|KommR|VetR|Techn\\s+R\\.|Dkfm\\.|Wirtschaftstreuh\u00e4nder\\.|Wirtschaftspr\u00fcfer\\.|MedR)?)?)\\s*,",
    "priority": 10,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "The 'names_at_start_of_text' rule is matching the first sentence of the document (e.g., 'Den Umstand', 'Ersuchen') because it assumes the first capitalized word is a name. This rule should be removed or heavily restricted to only match if the first word is followed by a comma and a second capitalized word, or if it matches a known list of common first names."
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "names_at_start_of_sentence",
    "description": "Matches names appearing at the very beginning of a sentence (after a period or start of text), often with titles like 'OStR', 'Dr.', 'Techn R', etc.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "(?:^|\\.\\s+)([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+(?:von\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?)?(?:\\s+(?:VetR|OSR|StR|Mag\\.|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|PhD|\\u00d6kR|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.\\s*MA|BA|RgR|KommR|MSc|Mag\\.a|LLM|LLB|BEd|Dipl\\.|MBA|Techn\\s+R\\.|Dkfm\\.|Wirtschaftstreuh\\u00e4nder\\.|Wirtschaftspr\\u00fcfer\\.|MedR)?)?)\\s+(?:,|\\s+(?:ist|hat|wurde|sind|haben|ist\\s+als|ist\\s+der|ist\\s+die))",
    "priority": 9,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "The 'names_at_start_of_sentence' rule is matching the entire sentence fragment (e.g., 'Herr Babette Reichenb\u00e4cher') because the capture group includes the title 'Herr'. The pattern should exclude the title from the capture group and only capture the name itself. Also, ensure the sentence structure implies a person is the subject, not just any capitalized word."
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "names_after_passive_voice",
    "description": "Matches names appearing after 'wurde' or 'wurden' in passive constructions, common in legal texts.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "(?:wurde|wurden)\\s+([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+(?:von\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?)?)",
    "priority": 8,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "The 'names_after_passive_voice' rule is matching titles like 'Frau' or 'Rechtsanwalt' as part of the name. The pattern should explicitly exclude titles (Herr, Frau, Rechtsanwalt, etc.) from the capture group and only capture the actual name following them."
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "names_with_herr_frau",
    "description": "Matches names following 'Herr' or 'Frau', ensuring the name is followed by a comma, address number, or end of sentence to avoid capturing non-person words.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "(?:Herr|Frau)\\s+([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*)(?=,|\\s+\\d+|\\s+\\w+\\s+\\d+|\\s+\\w+\\s+\\w+\\s+\\d+|\\s*$)",
    "priority": 11,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "This rule is correctly identifying names after 'Herr'/'Frau' but is missing the titles that follow the name (e.g., 'BEd'). The output template should be updated to include the title if it exists, or the rule should be adjusted to capture the full entity including the title if that is the desired output format."
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "names_in_case_context",
    "description": "Matches names appearing immediately after 'in der Beschwerdesache', 'in der Revisionssache', etc., capturing the full name including titles if present, stopping strictly before addresses or commas.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "(?:in\\s+der\\s+(?:Beschwerde|Revision|Finanzstraf|Verwaltungsstraf)sache(?:\\s+(?:der|des))?\\s+)([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+(?:von|van)\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?)(?=,|\\s+\\d+|\\s+\\w+\\s+\\d+|\\s+\\w+\\s+\\w+\\s+\\d+|\\s*$)",
    "priority": 15,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "This rule is identical to 'names_in_complaint_context' (id c1552200) and is causing duplicate matches. It is also matching partial names (e.g., 'Zlatan Grensenbach' instead of the full name with title). Consolidate this rule with the other complaint context rule and fix the capture group to include the full name and title if present."
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "names_after_family_terms",
    "description": "Matches names following family relationship terms, ensuring the match is a proper name (at least two capitalized words or a title + name) and excluding common non-person words.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "(?:Tochter|Mutter|Vater|Sohn|Ehefrau|Ehemann|Eltern|Kind|f\\u00fcr\\s+den|f\\u00fcr\\s+die|von)\\s+([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+von\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?)(?=,|\\s+\\d+|\\s+\\w+\\s+\\d+|\\s+\\w+\\s+\\w+\\s+\\d+|\\s*$)",
    "priority": 8,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "The 'names_after_family_terms' rule is matching common nouns like 'Jahre' because the pattern is too loose. Add a negative lookahead to exclude common German nouns that start with a capital letter and are not names. Also, ensure the rule only triggers in specific family contexts, not general prepositional phrases."
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "names_with_titles",
    "description": "Matches names preceded by German legal titles (Dr., Mag., Univ.-Prof., etc.) ensuring the full 'Title Name' span is captured, excluding the role word 'Richter' or 'Richterin' if present.",
    "format": "regex",
    "content": "(?:Richter|Richterin)\\s+(?:(?:Ing\\.\\s+)?(?:Univ\\.-Prof\\.|Univ\\.-Prof\\.in|Dr\\.|Dr\\.in|Mag\\.|Mag\\.a|Mag\\.Dr\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.|Priv\\.-Doz\\.in|Hon\\.-Prof\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\. Dr\\.|Hon\\.-Prof\\.in|Techn\\s+R\\.|Dkfm\\.|Wirtschaftstreuh\\u00e4nder\\.|Wirtschaftspr\\u00fcfer\\.|KommR|VetR|OSR|\\u00d6kR|StR|MedR|LLM|Bakk\\.\\s*techn\\.|Bakk\\.\\s*art\\.|Bakk\\.\\s*iur\\.)\\s+)?([A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+(?:\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)*(?:\\s+(?:von|van)\\s+[A-Z][a-z\\u00e4\\u00f6\\u00fc\\u00df]+)?)",
    "priority": 10,
    "output_template": {
      "text": "$1",
      "start": "$start",
      "end": "$end",
      "type": "person"
    },
    "output_key": "entities",
    "user_feedback": [
      "This rule is identical to 'names_with_complex_titles' (id 4406b6a9) and is causing duplicate matches. It is also matching partial names. Consolidate with the other rule and fix the pattern to require a full name, not just a single word."
    ]
  },
  {

WHAT MAKES A GOOD RULE:
- PRECISION OVER RECALL: A rule that matches 10 things correctly beats one that matches 100 with 20 wrong. Never sacrifice precision for recall. Missing a match is fixable later; a wrong match poisons results.
- GENERALIZE, DON'T MEMORIZE: Rules run on unseen text. Match the *structure* of values, not the exact strings you see in training data.
- USE CONTEXT: The same string can mean different things. Match surrounding words/punctuation and use capture groups ($1) to extract just the value.
- ONE RULE, ONE PATTERN: Multiple focused rules beat one giant regex. Each rule should have a clear, testable reason to exist.
- THINK ADVERSARIALLY: Before writing a rule, ask "what else could this match?" If the answer isn't empty, narrow the pattern or add context.


- Regex patterns (for structured extraction)

IMPORTANT: You must ONLY use the allowed formats listed above. Do NOT generate rules in other formats.
IMPORTANT: Do NOT include Python/code rules. Only return the listed formats.
IMPORTANT: Do NOT include spaCy rules. Only return the listed formats.

REGEX SYNTAX REFERENCE:
- \b word boundaries, (?:...) non-capturing groups, (a|b|c) alternation
- [A-Z] [a-z] \d \s character classes, +*?{n,m} quantifiers
- (?=...) lookahead, (?<=...) lookbehind — match context without consuming it
- (?i) case-insensitive flag

CAPTURE GROUPS ($1) vs FULL MATCH ($0):
- Use $0 in output_template when the entire regex match IS the value you want.
- Use $1 when you need surrounding context to disambiguate — put the value in a capture group.
  With "text": "$1", $start/$end auto-adjust to the group position automatically.

GOOD vs BAD PATTERNS (learn from these):

  BAD:  "\d+"                       — matches every number in the text, most will be wrong type
  GOOD: "(\d+)\s*(?:kg|lbs|oz)"    — only numbers followed by weight units; $1 captures the number

  BAD:  "Alice|Bob|Tokyo"            — memorizes training strings, misses all unseen values
  GOOD: "(?:Mr|Mrs|Dr)\.?\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)" — captures names after titles

  BAD:  "[A-Z][a-z]+"               — matches any capitalized word (way too broad)
  GOOD: "(?:in|from|at|near)\s+([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)*)" — locations after prepositions

  BAD:  "\b\w+@\w+\.\w+\b"         — matches email-like strings but also "user@v1.0"
  GOOD: "(?:email|contact)[:\s]+(\S+@\S+\.\w{2,})" — emails after a label; $1 captures address

  BAD:  one giant regex that handles 5 different sub-patterns
  GOOD: 5 separate rules, each handling one clear case with its own priority

IMPORTANT:
- Do NOT assume entities are capitalized; check the training data.
- Prefer structural patterns (\d+, [A-Z][a-z]+) over memorized alternations.
- When multiple entity types share similar text, use surrounding context to disambiguate.




SCHEMA-AWARE RULES:
For this task, rules must include an output_template that maps matches to the output schema.

Output schema:
entities: List[Entity]

Entity:
  text: string  # The matched text span
  start: integer  # Start character offset
  end: integer  # End character offset
  type: string  # Entity label

Each rule needs:
- pattern: The regex pattern to match
- output_template: JSON template for each match, using variables:
  - $0: Full match text
  - $start: Start character offset
  - $end: End character offset
  - $1, $2, ...: Capture groups (regex only)
- output_key: Which output array to populate (e.g., "entities")

$0 vs $1: Use $0 when the full match IS the value. Use a capture group ($1) when you
need surrounding context for disambiguation — $start/$end auto-adjust to the group.
  Example: "prefix(\w+)" with "text": "$1" extracts only the captured part.
